notes: Strongest moment: at turn 4 the examiner handled the user's double 'I don't know' exactly as intended — 'noted internally, no need for you to do anything there' — treating it as a finding rather than pressing to manufacture an answer, and the aporia brief faithfully logs both unknowns as unvalidated assumptions and open questions. Weakest moment: only two question types (clarification, assumptions) were exercised, understandable given the short session but limiting demonstrated type diversity. The turn-3 'as opposed to better docs' framing stays a legitimate assumption-probe rather than a recommendation, so no premature solutioning, and the verdict/brief are honestly grounded with no fabricated content.
